content.problemStatement:
**Long Answer - 5 marks**

You are given an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN sorted in **non-decreasing** order. Your task is to remove all the duplicates and find the sorted **increasing** array of distinct elements consisting of all distinct elements present in AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output two lines:



The first line should contain a single integer MMM - the size of the array.
The second line should contain MMM space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Distinct elements will be 555, 101010. So the array will be [5,10][5, 10][5,10].
Test case 222: Distinct elements will be 111, 555, 101010. So the array will be [1,5,10][1, 5, 10][1,5,10].
Test case 333: Distinct elements will be 444, 666, 888. So the array will be [4,6,8][4, 6, 8][4,6,8].
